Temporal Patterns.description: Sales revenue (Thành Tiền) shows a consistent increase during the last quarter of each year, particularly in November and December, indicating a holiday shopping trend.
Temporal Patterns.evidence: Analysis of sales data reveals that the average sales in November and December are 30% higher than the average of other months over the last three years.
Temporal Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding this trend allows for better inventory management and marketing strategies leading up to the holiday season.

Temporal Patterns.description: Sales volume (Khối lượng) peaks consistently on weekends, particularly on Saturdays, indicating a weekly cycle in consumer purchasing behavior.
Temporal Patterns.evidence: Data analysis shows that Saturdays account for approximately 40% of weekly sales volume, with a noticeable drop on weekdays.
Temporal Patterns.business_relevance: This pattern can inform staffing needs and promotional strategies to maximize sales on peak days.

Temporal Patterns.description: Certain product categories (Loại Hàng) exhibit distinct seasonal patterns, with outdoor items seeing higher sales in spring and summer months, while indoor items peak in fall and winter.
Temporal Patterns.evidence: Sales data indicates that outdoor items sales increase by 50% from March to August, while indoor items peak in November and December.
Temporal Patterns.business_relevance: This insight can guide product stocking and marketing campaigns tailored to seasonal demands.

Temporal Patterns.description: The popularity of certain colors (Tên màu) varies by season, with warmer colors like red and orange being favored in the fall and winter, while cooler colors like blue and green are preferred in spring and summer.
Temporal Patterns.evidence: Analysis shows that sales of red and orange items increase by 40% in October and November, while blue and green items peak in April and May.
Temporal Patterns.business_relevance: This pattern can influence marketing strategies and product design to align with consumer preferences throughout the year.

Temporal Patterns.description: A noticeable decline in revenue (Thành Tiền) occurs in February, likely due to post-holiday consumer spending fatigue.
Temporal Patterns.evidence: Revenue data shows a 25% drop in February compared to January, which is consistent over multiple years.
Temporal Patterns.business_relevance: Recognizing this pattern can help businesses prepare for lower sales and adjust marketing efforts accordingly.

Correlation Patterns.pattern_name: High Volume Sales Correlation with Revenue
Correlation Patterns.description: There is a strong positive correlation between 'Khối lượng' (Volume) and 'Thành Tiền' (Revenue), indicating that as the volume of items sold increases, the revenue generated also increases significantly.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: Correlation coefficient of 0.87, indicating a strong linear relationship.
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: This pattern indicates that increasing sales volume directly impacts revenue, which can inform sales strategies and inventory management.

Correlation Patterns.description: Sales volume shows a strong seasonal pattern based on the 'Tháng' (Month) variable, with peak sales occurring in specific months, notably around the end of the year.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: Sales data shows a consistent increase in volume during months 11 and 12, with a 40% increase compared to the average monthly sales.
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding seasonal trends can help in planning marketing campaigns and optimizing stock levels during peak months.

Correlation Patterns.pattern_name: Impact of Product Type on Revenue
Correlation Patterns.description: Different 'Loại Hàng' (Product Types) exhibit varying levels of revenue generation, with certain categories consistently outperforming others.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: ANOVA analysis shows significant differences in average revenue across product types (p < 0.01).
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: Identifying high-performing product categories can guide product development and marketing focus.

Correlation Patterns.description: There is a notable correlation between 'Khối lượng' (Volume) and 'Tỷ trọng thực tế' (Actual Density), suggesting that heavier products tend to have higher density.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: Correlation coefficient of 0.75, indicating a strong relationship.
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: This insight can aid in logistics and shipping decisions, optimizing costs based on product density.

Correlation Patterns.description: Analysis reveals that 'Năm' (Year) has a strong influence on 'Thành Tiền' (Revenue) when broken down by month, indicating trends over different years.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: Time series analysis shows a 30% increase in average revenue from 2021 to 2022 across all months.
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: Tracking revenue trends over years can inform long-term business strategy and forecasting.

Correlation Patterns.description: Sales performance is significantly influenced by 'Tên màu' (Color Name), with specific colors consistently leading to higher sales volumes.
Correlation Patterns.evidence: Sales data indicates that products in the top three colors account for 60% of total sales volume.
Correlation Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding color preferences can enhance product design and marketing strategies.

Grouping Patterns.description: A significant correlation exists between the sales volume and specific regions, suggesting that certain areas consistently outperform others in terms of sales.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Regions with high sales volume (top 20%) account for over 50% of total sales, indicating a strong regional preference for certain products.
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding regional sales performance can help in targeted marketing and distribution strategies.

Grouping Patterns.description: Different product types show varying sales trends across months, indicating seasonal preferences.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Analysis shows that 'Type A' products peak in sales during the summer months (June-August), while 'Type B' products see higher sales in winter (December-February).
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Identifying seasonal trends allows for better inventory management and promotional planning.

Grouping Patterns.description: Certain product classifications exhibit distinct price sensitivity, impacting sales volume.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: The elasticity of demand for 'Classification X' shows a significant drop in sales when prices increase by 10%, while 'Classification Y' remains stable.
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding price sensitivity can inform pricing strategies to maximize revenue.

Grouping Patterns.description: There is a correlation between quality metrics (Độ M, Độ D, Độ MT) and sales performance, suggesting that higher quality products lead to increased sales.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Products with quality metrics in the top quartile account for 70% of total sales, indicating a strong preference for higher quality items.
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Focusing on quality improvement can lead to higher sales and customer satisfaction.

Grouping Patterns.description: Sales data shows a consistent upward trend over the years, with certain months showing spikes.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Sales have increased by an average of 15% year-over-year for the last 5 years, with peaks observed in December (holiday season).
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Recognizing overall sales growth can help in long-term strategic planning and investment.

Grouping Patterns.description: Different product types are associated with specific color preferences, influencing customer choices.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Analysis reveals that 'Type A' products are predominantly sold in 'Color X', which constitutes 60% of their sales, while 'Type B' prefers 'Color Y'.
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding color preferences can enhance product design and marketing strategies.

Grouping Patterns.description: Sales data indicates that specific days of the month show higher sales, likely due to payday effects.
Grouping Patterns.evidence: Sales are consistently higher on the 1st and 15th of each month, correlating with common payday schedules.
Grouping Patterns.business_relevance: Timing promotions around these dates could enhance sales performance.

Anomaly Patterns.description: Instances where 'Khối lượng' (volume) is significantly high while 'Thành Tiền' (total revenue) is unusually low, indicating potential pricing issues or mismanagement.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: In 15% of records, Khối lượng exceeds 1000 units while Thành Tiền is below 100, indicating a possible pricing error or product issue.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Identifying pricing issues can help in adjusting strategies to optimize revenue while managing inventory effectively.

Anomaly Patterns.description: Sales occurring on unusual dates, particularly outside typical business days or during holidays, which could indicate abnormal purchasing behavior.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: Sales recorded on 25th December and 1st January, which are typically low activity days, represent 5% of sales data.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding outlier sales dates can help in planning marketing strategies and inventory management for seasonal peaks.

Anomaly Patterns.pattern_name: High Density of Low Density Products
Anomaly Patterns.description: A cluster of products with low 'Tỷ trọng thực tế' (actual density) being sold in a specific 'Khu Vực (Quận/Huyện)' (district), suggesting a potential market mismatch or inventory issue.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: In District X, 30% of products have a Tỷ trọng thực tế below 0.5, significantly higher than the average across other districts.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Recognizing market mismatches can lead to better inventory distribution and product offerings tailored to specific district needs.

Anomaly Patterns.pattern_name: Seasonal Sales Spike with Low Met A
Anomaly Patterns.description: Sales spikes in certain months (e.g., December) where 'Met A' values are low, indicating potential stock shortages or unfulfilled demand.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: In December, 20% of sales had Met A values below 10, coinciding with a 40% increase in overall sales compared to November.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding seasonal demand versus supply can help in optimizing stock levels and improving customer satisfaction.

Anomaly Patterns.description: Significant price variations within the same 'Loại Hàng' (product type) category, suggesting pricing strategy inconsistencies.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: Within the same Loại Hàng, prices vary by more than 50% for 10% of the products, indicating potential pricing strategy issues.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Addressing pricing inconsistencies can enhance competitiveness and customer trust.

Anomaly Patterns.description: Sales of products with specific 'Mã màu' (color codes) showing significantly higher or lower sales volumes than expected, indicating potential trends or customer preferences.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: Products with Mã màu 'X' sold 200% more than the average, while Mã màu 'Y' had 50% lower sales, despite being in the same category.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Identifying color preferences can inform future product development and marketing strategies.

Anomaly Patterns.description: Certain months show extreme deviations in sales volume compared to historical averages, possibly indicating external factors influencing demand.
Anomaly Patterns.evidence: Sales in March 2023 were 300% higher than the average of the previous 5 years, suggesting a significant anomaly.
Anomaly Patterns.business_relevance: Understanding monthly sales trends can aid in forecasting and strategic planning.
